spans.text: No Builder of the pow’rful Rhime
to subjection awe , Free as the native air they draw
If aught their wishes can befriend
you , whose glorious deed Already meets so high a need
to crush , or to control The native Ardour of his Soul
control The native Ardour of his Soul
The native Ardour of his Soul
Thy Colleague in the Soldier’s toil , Oft ’ hast thou seen him reap the spoil ; And late his glowing Cheek confessed , How civic praises
that hateful race ) There is a man , whose grov’ling soul Each meaner motive can control , Who from the sordid love of pelf , Will sell his Country , — sell himself
Who from the sordid love of pelf , Will sell his Country
to prostitute his Daughter , Than of his Liberties make slaughter
That faintly speaks your high renown
circling wave , Your gardens and your walls
